own_review_points: Manifest 'Multi-Release: true' must be a hard prerequisite/primary trigger; classpath-attribute detection should live inside that gate (human ref: AbstractOsgiCompilerMojo:390)
Auto-setting Multi-Release header is wrong — it is a prerequisite the user must opt into, not something the build sets (human ref: PackagePluginMojo:392)
getMultiReleaseVersion should return OptionalInt not Integer (human ref: SourceFolderClasspathEntry:35)
Docs/README repeatedly use wrong attribute name and over-claim flexible folder naming semantics (human ref README:5/31)
Test only checks verifyErrorFreeLog, does not assert META-INF/versions/N contents
Test source folders with both test=true and release=N could be misrouted into main bundle versions output
notes: v8's test-coverage point (no assertion on META-INF/versions/N contents) is a genuine, grounded finding. The test=true interaction point is plausible but unverifiable from truncation. The rest is largely site.xml menu-placement and javadoc-wording nits plus restating that the fallback is fine. It misses the entire substance of the human review: the manifest-header-as-prerequisite design issue, the auto-set-header bug, the OptionalInt request, and the attribute-naming doc errors.
